blocks.title: about
blocks.body:
Biography

Arash Famiilirani is a Montreal-based electroacoustic composer and sound artist whose work

explores the boundaries between perception, time, and minimal sonic materials. A graduate of

Concordia University’s Bachelor of Fine Arts program in Electroacoustic Studies, Famiilirani’s

practice draws from both experimental composition and attentive listening, transforming subtle

gestures and everyday sounds into immersive auditory experiences. His recent works

investigate psychoacoustic phenomena and the tension between structure and stillness,

inviting audiences to engage with sound as a living, perceptual field.

Work Experience

Audio Engineer

Canyon Entertainment Group — Montreal, QC (2025)

• Edited, mixed, and mastered weekly podcast episodes, maintaining broadcast-ready

sound quality.

• Applied advanced processing (EQ, compression, noise reduction) to improve clarity and

optimize mixes for multiple listening platforms.

• Enhanced storytelling with custom-designed transitions and immersive sound effects.

• Authored metadata, show notes, and publishing documentation, streamlining digital

distribution.

• Conducted ongoing market and trend research to adjust audio styles for maximum

audience engagement.

• Provided technical troubleshooting and workflow improvements, helping the media

team adopt efficient production practices.

Freelance Audio Producer & Sound Engineer

Montreal, QC (2014 – Present)

• Designed and implemented interactive audio systems in Wwise for games and

multimedia projects, ensuring seamless integration with gameplay.

• Created dynamic soundscapes and adaptive music scores tailored to narrative and player

experience.

• Coordinated full production cycles, including budgets, schedules, logistics, and team

communication.• Delivered high-quality recordings using professional studio equipment and

DAWs

(Reaper, Logic Pro).

• Mixed and mastered music, dialogue, and SFX to commercial-ready standards.

Developed efficient workflows and documentation for technical processes, ensuring projects

stayed within scope and met deadlines.

Associate Producer & Production Assistant (Various Projects)Concordia University — Montreal, QC (2018 – 2024)

• Assisted in planning and executing 10+ theatre and musical productions, coordinating

schedules, venue bookings, and multi-department communications.

• Operated live sound systems during performances, delivering balanced, reliable mixes

across complex technical setups.

• Designed multitrack recording workflows and provided on-the-fly troubleshooting to

ensure smooth technical operations.

• Drafted technical and budget-related sections of successful grant applications, helping

secure production funding.

• Supported marketing and audience outreach with social media strategy and promotional

material design.

• Gained extensive practical experience in stage management, creative collaboration,

and documentation of production workflows.

Audio Producer & Sound Designer

CJLO 1690 AM Radio — Montreal, QC (2019 – 2020)

• Produced station IDs, bumpers, and promotional ads, elevating the station’s brand

identity.

• Composed original music and designed custom SFX tailored to specific radio

programming.• Managed and catalogued a large audio asset library, creating workflows that

improved

accessibility and long-term efficiency.

• Applied professional post-production techniques (noise reduction, EQ balancing,

compression, limiting) to deliver consistent broadcast quality.

• Collaborated closely with on-air talent and producers to develop engaging content,

ensuring all assets were delivered on schedule.
